judgment.summary: Oracle shows strong growth and profitability metrics, but carries significant debt and negative free cash flow.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimate by 5.7%; next earnings date is 2026-06-10.
positives: Revenue growth 21.7% YoY and earnings growth 24.5% YoY indicate robust top-line and bottom-line expansion.
Profit margin 25.3% and Return on Equity 57.6% demonstrate strong profitability and efficient use of shareholder capital.
Forward P/E of 23.38x is attractive relative to trailing P/E of 33.71x, suggesting expected earnings growth.
Consensus analyst rating is 'buy' with a mean target price of $244.03, indicating positive sentiment from the sell-side.
negatives: Debt/Equity ratio of 415.26 is extremely high, indicating significant leverage.
Total debt of $162.16B dwarfs its total cash of $39.13B.
Free cash flow is negative at $-22.30B TTM, suggesting the company is burning cash.
Beta of 1.54 indicates higher volatility than the overall market.

news.paragraph: Oracle is expanding its clinical research capabilities through AI integration, a move that Wedbush analysts view positively, maintaining a bullish stance and highlighting strong demand visibility. While a competitor's CEO is also addressing the AI era, Oracle's strategic AI integration in a key sector like healthcare research suggests a proactive approach to leveraging new technologies. The upcoming earnings report will be a key catalyst to watch.

headlines.title: Workday’s Returning CEO Has a Plan to Survive the AI Era
Oracle Corporation (ORCL) Expands Clinical Research with AI Integration
Wedbush Remains Bullish On Oracle (ORCL), Says Investors Are Overlooking Demand Visibility
